title: Air Cavalry Trooper - Ia Drang Valley, 1965
startLocation: An Khe base camp, Central Highlands, South Vietnam
opening: The Hueys are turning and burning on the strip before dawn, sixteen of them, rotor wash flattening the elephant grass and slapping your fatigues like wet laundry. Sergeant Esposito walks the stick line with two fingers up - two minutes. Intel says there's a People's Army regiment somewhere at the foot of the Chu Pong massif, near a clearing on the map marked X-Ray. Nobody in this battalion has fought the North Vietnamese regulars before. Nobody anywhere has done what you're about to do: ride helicopters into a fight and ride them out again. Mickelson, nineteen, leans over and shouts the only question that matters: 'How many days you got left?' You're climbing aboard. The skids leave the ground.

squad.name: Sgt. Tony Esposito
squad.trait: Korea veteran, calm as concrete, learns every replacement's hometown on day one

squad.name: Sp4. Marcus Whitfield
squad.trait: carries the radio and the squad's luck; quotes his grandmother under fire

squad.name: Pfc. Danny Mickelson
squad.trait: draftee from Minnesota, counts days out loud, braver than he believes

squad.name: Doc Tran
squad.trait: grew up in Saigon, translates for the squad, sleeps less than anyone

timelinePressure: 1965-11-14 Air assault into LZ X-Ray at the foot of Chu Pong mountain
1965-11-17 The march to LZ Albany and the ambush in the tall grass
1966-01-01 Operation Masher looms - the war of attrition begins in earnest
